steps.title: Isolate the absolute value expression
steps.content:
Start with $-2|3x + 7| + 8 = -6$. Subtract 8 from both sides:
$$-2|3x + 7| = -14$$
Divide both sides by $-2$:
$$|3x + 7| = 7$$

steps.title: Apply the absolute value definition to find both solutions
steps.content:
The equation $|3x + 7| = 7$ means the expression inside is either $7$ or $-7$.

Case 1: When $3x + 7 = 7$:
$$3x = 0$$
$$x = 0$$
Case 2: When $3x + 7 = -7$:
$$3x = -14$$
$$x = -\frac{14}{3}$$
The solutions are $x = 0$ and $x = -\frac{14}{3}$. That confirms C is correct.
